title: MITRE ATT&CK - Brute Force
content: Brute force activity involves repeated authentication attempts against one or more accounts. SOC teams usually validate source reputation, targeted accounts, lockout events, and whether any attempt eventually succeeded.

title: MITRE ATT&CK - Command and Scripting Interpreter
content: Command and scripting interpreters such as PowerShell are common execution mechanisms. Analysts typically review process lineage, encoded arguments, parent processes, and follow-on persistence or credential access behavior.

title: Sigma Rule Guidance - Suspicious PowerShell Encoded Command
content: Sigma detections for encoded PowerShell commands often look for the EncodedCommand flag, unusual parent processes like Office apps, and execution outside normal admin tooling baselines.

title: SOC Playbook - Suspicious Authentication Activity
content: A typical authentication investigation playbook checks account criticality, MFA results, source geography, repeated failures, success after failures, and nearby privilege changes before deciding on containment.

title: SOC Playbook - Potential Data Exfiltration
content: For possible exfiltration, analysts review outbound transfer volume, archive creation, cloud destinations, affected data owners, and whether the user or host recently showed signs of compromise.

title: MITRE ATT&CK - Remote Services
content: Remote services can be abused for lateral movement. Analysts usually check the source host, the target host, authentication method, spawned processes, and whether service accounts were involved.
